t1.html:
Region T1

Verdantglade

1st class · Warrior & Mage

Investment

Grades assume a built-out account. The Sorcerer and Archmage only reach their S-tier ceiling with heavy investment, while the Warrior line (Duelist, Berserker) hits its grades far sooner. For low to medium spenders, the Warrior line returns more power per resource than the Mage line, making it the stronger value pick until your account can fund a caster's full scaling.

Early game

Verdantglade clears with any class. There is no meaningful gap at this tier. Pick the class you want to play long-term and focus on leveling.

Updated: May 20, 2026 · Patch 1.0

Launch-Week Tier List

Sword x Staff launched globally on May 19. This list reflects EOG's launch-week read on Verdantglade Fantomons. Stats unlock at Fantomon levels 20, 50, 70, and 100, so a Fantomon's effective tier shifts as you level it. Use this to shortlist, then test against your own bottleneck.

Fantomons are the pet companions that unlock at account level 50 through the giant tree at your home base. Each provides passive stats and joins the active battle line. Most players run four core Fantomons leveled evenly rather than dumping resources into one.

Tier Overview

Mythical

  • Aegiswing Attack · Main DPS bond

    Damage-leaning trait set. Common pick for any Duelist or Sorcerer team that wants ceiling damage.

  • Nyxarchon Attack · Main DPS bond

    The other Mythical attacker. Slot together with Aegiswing for stacked attack-side traits.

Healer / Charm

  • Mandragora Charm / Heal · Sustain bond

    Adds heal procs to Sage rotations. Worth slotting if you are running guild boss DoT phases.

  • Herbote Charm / Heal · Sustain bond

    Pairs with Mandragora for redundant heal coverage. Sage primary, Sorcerer secondary.

Level Breakpoints

Stat tiers from a single Fantomon do not unlock smoothly. They land at four discrete levels.

Per-level stat grants
Level 20 Minor stat (Damage Bonus, Resistance, etc.)
Level 50 Major stat (Crit Rate, Accuracy, Block Rate, etc.)
Level 70 Second minor stat
Level 100 Second major stat

F2P plan: Get every Fantomon to level 20 first. Then raise the four that match your class trait (Crit Rate for DPS, Block Rate for Knight) to level 50 for the major stat unlock. Push to 100 only on the ones you actually use.

Whale plan: Skip the spread. Max the Fantomons you actually run. The per-stat payoff stacks linearly, so the cap is more about which roster you commit to than which one you pull next.

2nd class advancement at Lv.44

Investment

Grades assume a built-out account. The Sorcerer and Archmage only reach their S-tier ceiling with heavy investment, while the Warrior line (Duelist, Berserker) hits its grades far sooner. For low to medium spenders, the Warrior line returns more power per resource than the Mage line, making it the stronger value pick until your account can fund a caster's full scaling.

Class tier list Cinder Ridge

S Top pick
Sorcerer Sorcerer line Sorcerer Sorcerer line S Overall

The fastest content-pusher of the tier: a Fire/Light/Water spread keeps Elemental Harmony maxed while it carpets whole rooms from range, deleting packs before they reach it.
A Strong
Duelist Duelist line Duelist Duelist line A Overall

Aggressive melee burst that shreds a pack the moment it arrives - a half-step behind the Sorcerer only because it has to close the gap the caster clears from range.

 Knight Knight line Knight Knight line A Overall

Bulletproof through any pull, but its kit is built to hold a line, not push content - a solo Knight clears noticeably slower than the DPS lines, so safety alone earns an A.
B Solid
Sage Sage line Sage Sage line B Overall

Support first: its damage tools double as Erosion/Weaken applicators rather than nukes, so it kills slowly and pushes content the slowest of the four. Strong only once it has a party to heal.

Best solo clearer at Aqualis - Hunter's Judgment drags a pack into Eclipse Slash's multi-hit burst while Blade of Judgment detonates Marks across the pile.

 Archmage Sorcerer line Archmage Sorcerer line S PvE

Ranged AoE melts packs from full screen - Divine Wrath's barrage plus Meteoric Flames clears as fast as anything that isn't a Berserker pull.
Archmage Sorcerer line Archmage Sorcerer line S Dragon & Chaos

Rare for a non-Duelist: its AoE-plus-element synergy actually pushes the sustained DPS check, so it holds S where the tank line collapses.

Bulk plus one enormous nuke wins duels of attrition - it outlasts and then one-shots, so survivability translates straight into arena value.

 Arcanist Sage line Arcanist Sage line A PvP

Brings burst and a pack-pull, but a teamfight rewards the AoE zoning an Archmage provides more than single-target dives.

 Arcanist Sage line Arcanist Sage line A 4v4

Chip damage and control support the team - it enables more than it kills, which a coordinated 4v4 rewards.

Region T4

4th class advancement

Investment

Grades assume a built-out account. The Sorcerer and Archmage only reach their S-tier ceiling with heavy investment, while the Warrior line (Duelist, Berserker) hits its grades far sooner. Class tier list Loong Haven

Grade PvEDragon & ChaosPvP4v4
SSS Crown
Conqueror Duelist line Conqueror Duelist line SSS PvE

Peak of the line - fire-fuelled multi-hits clear packs faster than anything else at Loong Haven.
Conqueror Duelist line Conqueror Duelist line SSS Dragon & Chaos

Unlike the rest of the field its burst is sustained, so it pushes the Dragon DPS check from the front and holds the crown.
Conqueror Duelist line Conqueror Duelist line SSS PvP

Burst plus lifesteal wins duels outright - it kills before the trade matters.
Conqueror Duelist line Conqueror Duelist line SSS 4v4

AoE that ignores a slice of defense carries teamfights, turning grouped enemies into the ideal target.

 Guardian Knight line Guardian Knight line SSS 4v4

The best frontline anchor in the tier - Taunt and space control let it dictate where the teamfight happens.
S Top pick
Destroyer Sorcerer line Destroyer Sorcerer line S PvE

Keeps the line's ranged-AoE identity, staying a fast, safe pack clearer from full distance.
-
Guardian Knight line Guardian Knight line S PvP

Bulk plus the Light Sword Array counter wins attrition duels, where outlasting the opponent is the whole game.

 Destroyer Sorcerer line Destroyer Sorcerer line S PvP

Multi-element burst with chaining damage duels well, punishing anyone who can't close the gap.

 Dominator Sage line Dominator Sage line S PvP

Control plus summon pressure wins duels - a decoy and chaos damage tie the opponent down while it chips them out.
-
A Strong
Guardian Knight line Guardian Knight line A PvE

Clears safely behind shields and counters, but not at the pace of the DPS lines - a solid A rather than a top pick.

 Dominator Sage line Dominator Sage line A PvE

Where the support line finally carries real damage - summons and Dark bursts clear PvE at a genuine pace for the first time.
-
-
Destroyer Sorcerer line Destroyer Sorcerer line A 4v4

Strong AoE, but it brings less hard control than the rivals, so it supports the teamfight rather than dominating it.

 Dominator Sage line Dominator Sage line A 4v4

Summons and team buffs make it a strong enabler, lifting the whole squad more than its own damage suggests.
B Solid
-
Destroyer Sorcerer line Destroyer Sorcerer line B Dragon & Chaos

Pushes the check solidly but not from the front - its synergy lags the Conqueror here, so it slips to a mid grade.

-

Guardian Knight line Guardian Knight line D Dragon & Chaos

Same wall as ever: a defensive, counter-based kit can't keep up with the Dragon DPS race, so it bottoms out.

 Dominator Sage line Dominator Sage line D Dragon & Chaos

Still debuff- and sustain-reliant, so it can't move the Dragon DPS check fast enough and falls to the bottom.

Updated: May 20, 2026 · Patch 1.0

Launch-Week Tier List

Sword x Staff launched globally on May 19. This list reflects EOG's launch-week read on Verdantglade Fantomons. Stats unlock at Fantomon levels 20, 50, 70, and 100, so a Fantomon's effective tier shifts as you level it. Use this to shortlist, then test against your own bottleneck.

Fantomons are the pet companions that unlock at account level 50 through the giant tree at your home base. Each provides passive stats and joins the active battle line. Most players run four core Fantomons leveled evenly rather than dumping resources into one.

Tier Overview

Mythical

  • Aegiswing Attack · Main DPS bond

    Damage-leaning trait set. Common pick for any Duelist or Sorcerer team that wants ceiling damage.

  • Nyxarchon Attack · Main DPS bond

    The other Mythical attacker. Slot together with Aegiswing for stacked attack-side traits.

Healer / Charm

  • Mandragora Charm / Heal · Sustain bond

    Adds heal procs to Sage rotations. Worth slotting if you are running guild boss DoT phases.

  • Herbote Charm / Heal · Sustain bond

    Pairs with Mandragora for redundant heal coverage. Sage primary, Sorcerer secondary.

Level Breakpoints

Stat tiers from a single Fantomon do not unlock smoothly. They land at four discrete levels.

Per-level stat grants
Level 20 Minor stat (Damage Bonus, Resistance, etc.)
Level 50 Major stat (Crit Rate, Accuracy, Block Rate, etc.)
Level 70 Second minor stat
Level 100 Second major stat

F2P plan: Get every Fantomon to level 20 first. Then raise the four that match your class trait (Crit Rate for DPS, Block Rate for Knight) to level 50 for the major stat unlock. Push to 100 only on the ones you actually use.

Whale plan: Skip the spread. Max the Fantomons you actually run. The per-stat payoff stacks linearly, so the cap is more about which roster you commit to than which one you pull next.

Updated: May 20, 2026 · Patch 1.0

Launch-Week Tier List

Sword x Staff launched globally on May 19. This list reflects EOG's launch-week read on Verdantglade Fantomons. Stats unlock at Fantomon levels 20, 50, 70, and 100, so a Fantomon's effective tier shifts as you level it. Use this to shortlist, then test against your own bottleneck.

Fantomons are the pet companions that unlock at account level 50 through the giant tree at your home base. Each provides passive stats and joins the active battle line. Most players run four core Fantomons leveled evenly rather than dumping resources into one.

Tier Overview

Mythical

  • Aegiswing Attack · Main DPS bond

    Damage-leaning trait set. Common pick for any Duelist or Sorcerer team that wants ceiling damage.

  • Nyxarchon Attack · Main DPS bond

    The other Mythical attacker. Slot together with Aegiswing for stacked attack-side traits.

Healer / Charm

  • Mandragora Charm / Heal · Sustain bond

    Adds heal procs to Sage rotations. Worth slotting if you are running guild boss DoT phases.

  • Herbote Charm / Heal · Sustain bond

    Pairs with Mandragora for redundant heal coverage. Sage primary, Sorcerer secondary.

Level Breakpoints

Stat tiers from a single Fantomon do not unlock smoothly. They land at four discrete levels.

Per-level stat grants
Level 20 Minor stat (Damage Bonus, Resistance, etc.)
Level 50 Major stat (Crit Rate, Accuracy, Block Rate, etc.)
Level 70 Second minor stat
Level 100 Second major stat

F2P plan: Get every Fantomon to level 20 first. Then raise the four that match your class trait (Crit Rate for DPS, Block Rate for Knight) to level 50 for the major stat unlock. Push to 100 only on the ones you actually use.

Whale plan: Skip the spread. Max the Fantomons you actually run. The per-stat payoff stacks linearly, so the cap is more about which roster you commit to than which one you pull next.
